visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 10–50 m (33–164 ft) tall, a few meters wide; can exceed 1 km (0.6 mi) tall
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: rotating pocket of air with a burning core

domain_specific_visual.vortex_intensity: can reach wind speeds over 200 km/h (120 mph)

functional_attributes.primary_function: a natural phenomenon resulting from fire-induced wind conditions
secondary_functions: aids in wildfire propagation by lifting burning materials

relationships.description: Fire whirls are often spawned by wildfires.
Fire whirls can be associated with pyrocumulonimbus clouds.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: documented throughout history, notable in 1923 Great Kantō earthquake
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: global, particularly in areas prone to wildfires
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the destructive power of fire and wind
